labels.name: Input
labels.desc: Data coming in from a user
labels.example:
Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
String name = sc.nextLine();

labels.name: Variable
labels.desc: A named holder for a single value

labels.name: Condition
labels.desc: A decision or branch based on a value

labels.name: Loop
labels.desc: Something that repeats one or more times

labels.name: Method
labels.desc: A named, reusable block of behavior on a class

labels.name: Class
labels.desc: A blueprint for creating objects with shared structure and behavior

labels.name: Object
labels.desc: An instance of a class with its own data

labels.name: Computation
labels.desc: A calculation that produces a result
labels.example:
double avg = total / count;
double root = Math.sqrt(value);

labels.name: Encapsulation
labels.desc: Keeping internal details protected and only reachable through controlled access

prompts.title: Vehicle Blueprint
prompts.statement: A used car dealership needs a consistent way to represent any vehicle in their system. Every vehicle carries the same information — an ID, make and model, color, odometer reading, and price — and none of that should be directly reachable or changeable without going through the right channels.

expectedTags.phrase: a consistent way to represent any vehicle in their system
expectedTags.explanation: The dealership needs a single template that defines what every vehicle looks like — a shared blueprint that any individual vehicle can be built from.

expectedTags.phrase: Every vehicle carries the same information — an ID, make and model, color, odometer reading, and price
expectedTags.explanation: Each individual vehicle is its own record with its own copy of those values. Each piece of information is a named holder for a single value belonging to that vehicle.

expectedTags.phrase: none of that should be directly reachable or changeable without going through the right channels
expectedTags.explanation: The vehicle's details are kept protected from the outside. The only way to read or update them is through a controlled path — a defined channel the rest of the program must use.

codeSnippets.encapsulation:
private int id;
private double price;

prompts.title: Vehicle Inventory
prompts.statement: The dealership lot holds a maximum of 20 cars. Six vehicles are already on the lot when the program starts, and the system always needs to know the current count.

expectedTags.phrase: holds a maximum of 20 cars
expectedTags.explanation: The lot has a hard upper limit, so the collection that holds the vehicles must be fixed in size from the start.

expectedTags.phrase: Six vehicles are already on the lot when the program starts
expectedTags.explanation: Each of those six vehicles is an individual record placed into the collection — six distinct entries that occupy six slots from the beginning.

expectedTags.phrase: the system always needs to know the current count
expectedTags.explanation: The running total must be tracked as a named value that reflects how many vehicles are present at any given moment.

codeSnippets.computation: count++; // incremented after each vehicle is added

prompts.title: Menu-Driven Program
prompts.statement: A dealership front desk application shows staff a list of actions to choose from. Whatever number they pick drives what the program does next, and the menu comes back after every action until they choose to quit.

expectedTags.phrase: shows staff a list of actions to choose from
expectedTags.explanation: The available options must be displayed so staff can see them, and then the program must wait for the staff member to indicate their selection.

expectedTags.phrase: Whatever number they pick drives what the program does next
expectedTags.explanation: Each possible choice leads to a different path — the program branches based on which number was entered.

expectedTags.phrase: the menu comes back after every action until they choose to quit
expectedTags.explanation: The whole sequence repeats — show, pick, respond — and that cycle continues until a specific choice signals it should stop.

prompts.title: Vehicle Search
prompts.statement: A customer wants to find vehicles by make and model. The program asks what they're looking for, checks every vehicle on the lot, and reports back anything that fits.

expectedTags.phrase: asks what they're looking for
expectedTags.explanation: The program must prompt the customer with a question, then wait to receive what they type in response.

expectedTags.phrase: checks every vehicle on the lot
expectedTags.explanation: Every slot in the collection must be visited one by one so none are missed during the search.

expectedTags.phrase: reports back anything that fits
expectedTags.explanation: Each vehicle is evaluated against what the customer entered — only the ones that match get surfaced and shown.

prompts.title: Add a Vehicle
prompts.statement: A new car just arrived at the lot. A staff member enters its details, but if the lot is already at capacity the program should say so and stop there. If there's room, the vehicle joins the rest and the running total updates.

expectedTags.phrase: if the lot is already at capacity
expectedTags.explanation: Before anything else happens, the current count must be compared against the maximum — a decision that determines which path follows.

expectedTags.phrase: the program should say so and stop there
expectedTags.explanation: When the lot is full, the staff member must be informed and no further action should take place — the program takes the branch that ends with a message.

codeSnippets.output: System.out.println("Inventory is full — cannot add vehicle.");

expectedTags.phrase: A staff member enters its details
expectedTags.explanation: The details of the new vehicle come from the staff member typing them in.

expectedTags.phrase: the vehicle joins the rest
expectedTags.explanation: A new individual vehicle record is placed into the collection alongside the existing ones.

codeSnippets.object: new Vehicle(id, makeModel, color, odometer, price)
codeSnippets.array: inventory[count] = new Vehicle(id, makeModel, color, odometer, price);

expectedTags.phrase: the running total updates
expectedTags.explanation: The value tracking how many vehicles are on the lot must be recalculated and updated to reflect the addition.

codeSnippets.variable: int count = 6; // starts at 6, updated after each addition

prompts.title: Library Book
prompts.statement: A library needs a consistent way to represent any book in its collection. Each book has an ID, ISBN, and title, and at any moment is either on the shelf or checked out to a patron. Its details should not be reachable from outside without going through the right channels, and checking out or returning a book should work the same way every time.

expectedTags.phrase: a consistent way to represent any book in its collection
expectedTags.explanation: The library needs a single template that defines what every book looks like — a shared blueprint that any individual book can be built from.

expectedTags.phrase: Each book has an ID, ISBN, and title, and at any moment is either on the shelf or checked out to a patron
expectedTags.explanation: Each individual book is its own record with its own values for those details. Each piece of information — including its current status — is a named holder for a single value.

expectedTags.phrase: Its details should not be reachable from outside without going through the right channels
expectedTags.explanation: The book's information is kept protected so that nothing outside can access or change it directly — any interaction must come through a controlled path.

expectedTags.phrase: checking out or returning a book should work the same way every time
expectedTags.explanation: These two transitions are defined behaviors that apply consistently to any book — reusable actions that always follow the same steps.

prompts.title: Check Out a Book
prompts.statement: A library patron wants to borrow a book. The system shows only what is currently available, the patron says who they are and which book they want, and the system finds it and records it under their name.

expectedTags.phrase: shows only what is currently available
expectedTags.explanation: Every book in the collection must be examined one by one, a decision made about whether it qualifies, and the ones that do get displayed to the patron.

expectedTags.phrase: the patron says who they are and which book they want
expectedTags.explanation: Both pieces of information — the patron's name and their selection — come from what the patron types in.

expectedTags.phrase: the system finds it
expectedTags.explanation: The collection must be visited one entry at a time, checking each book until the matching one is identified.

expectedTags.phrase: records it under their name
expectedTags.explanation: The individual book record is updated through its defined checkout behavior, which ties the patron's name to that specific book.

codeSnippets.object: books[i] // the specific Book object being updated

prompts.title: Validated Input
prompts.statement: A math utility needs a valid positive number before it can do anything useful. The program keeps asking until it gets one, telling the user why each rejected entry doesn't qualify, and then delivers the result once a good number is provided.

expectedTags.phrase: a valid positive number
expectedTags.explanation: A number needs to come from the user — it must be provided externally and held so the program can evaluate and eventually use it.

codeSnippets.input:
System.out.print("Enter a positive number: ");
number = scanner.nextDouble();

expectedTags.phrase: keeps asking until it gets one
expectedTags.explanation: The request for a number repeats, and the cycle only ends when the value provided meets the requirement — a decision determines whether to continue or stop.

expectedTags.phrase: telling the user why each rejected entry doesn't qualify
expectedTags.explanation: When the number fails the check, the user must be shown a message explaining it — a response that only happens on the branch where the value was unacceptable.

expectedTags.phrase: delivers the result once a good number is provided
expectedTags.explanation: After a valid number is in hand, a value is derived from it mathematically and then shown to the user.
